backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor for the Cricket Premier backend, managing teams, matches, tournaments, and user profiles with role-based access control (admin/user) enforced via a MixinAuthorization mixin.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping implemented features (Internet Identity auth, IPL/BBL/Domestic tournament modes, etc.) to their source files, components, and test IDs with confidence ratings.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document providing one-line summaries of each frontend source file, covering App.tsx, all five page components, and the match simulation engine.
frontend/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS stylesheet defining CSS custom properties for light and dark theme color tokens (background, foreground, primary, chart colors, sidebar, etc.).
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML entry point for the React app, mounting the root div and loading the main.tsx module script with the title 'Cricket Game'.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that sets up TanStack Router with five routes, wraps all pages in an AuthGuard (redirects to LoginScreen if unauthenticated, shows ProfileSetupModal if no profile), and renders a shared Header/Footer layout.
frontend/src/components/CommentaryFeed.t: Scrollable commentary feed component that displays the 20 most recent ball events with color-coded badges and text for wickets, sixes, fours, and normal deliveries.
frontend/src/components/DashboardCard.ts: Reusable navigation card component for the dashboard that displays an icon, title, description, optional badge, and navigates to a given route on click with hover animations.
frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx: App footer component showing the Cricket Premier branding, copyright year, and a 'Built with caffeine.ai' attribution link.
frontend/src/components/Header.tsx: Sticky navigation header with logo, nav links for cricket tournament sections, and a user dropdown menu supporting logout via Internet Identity.
frontend/src/components/LoginScreen.tsx: Full-page login screen displaying a stadium banner, feature highlights, and an Internet Identity authentication button.
frontend/src/components/MatchResultSumma: Displays the final match result including winner, scorecards for both innings, top scorer, best bowler, and play-again/go-back actions.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetupModa: Modal dialog prompting new users to enter a display name and save their profile before accessing the app.
frontend/src/components/Scorecard.tsx: Live scorecard component showing the current innings score, run rate, active batsmen, current bowler, and optional chase target.
frontend/src/components/TeamSelector.tsx: Grid-based team picker that renders selectable team cards with flag, name, and code, optionally excluding one team from the list.
frontend/src/components/TournamentPoints: React component that computes and displays a cricket tournament points table (played, won, lost, NRR, points) from match results, highlighting top-N qualifying teams with badges.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom React hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor (authenticated or anonymous) using React Query, reinitializing dependent queries whenever the identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an InternetIdentityProvider and useInternetIdentity hook for managing ICP Internet Identity authentication state, including login, logout, and identity persistence via AuthClient.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Centralizes React Query hooks for all backend data operations (user profiles, teams, tournament teams, matches), wrapping actor calls with useQuery and useMutation.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS file that imports Google Fonts, configures Tailwind with a cricket-themed dark color palette using OKLCH variables, and defines custom utility classes for stadium-style UI elements.
frontend/src/lib/cricketData.ts: Static data file defining TypeScript interfaces and pre-populated arrays of international cricket teams with player batting/bowling ratings used to seed the application.
frontend/src/lib/matchSimulator.ts: Provides cricket match simulation logic including ball-by-ball outcome generation with weighted probabilities, commentary generation, and full innings simulation based on batting/bowling ratings.
frontend/src/main.tsx: React app entry point that wraps the App component with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider before mounting to the DOM.
frontend/src/pages/BBLTournament.tsx: BBL tournament page that simulates a round-robin group stage and finals bracket using BBL team data, records results to the backend, and displays a points table.
frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx: Main dashboard page displaying game mode navigation cards (International, IPL, BBL, Domestic) and a list of recent completed matches fetched from the backend.
frontend/src/pages/DomesticTournament.ts: Domestic tournament page supporting Ranji Trophy, Sheffield Shield, and County Championship leagues, simulating round-robin matches and recording results per selected league.
frontend/src/pages/InternationalMatch.ts: React page component that manages the full lifecycle of an international cricket match simulation, including team selection, ball-by-ball play, auto-simulation, and result display across setup/playing/result phases.
frontend/src/pages/IPLTournament.tsx: IPL tournament page that simulates a round-robin group stage and knockout rounds for IPL teams, persists results to the backend, and shows standings and champion.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility module providing functions to read URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routing, persist them in sessionStorage, and clean sensitive params from the URL bar.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the default theme with custom fonts, OKLCH-based color tokens, cricket-specific colors (pitch, gold), box shadows, and animations, plus typography, container-queries, and animate plugins.
project_state.json: Project state manifest describing the cricket game app's architecture, existing features (authentication, IPL, BBL, domestic, international modes), known issues, and required image assets.
